segment.source: Access8Math feature
segment.target: Access8Math feature

segment.source: This NVDA addon enhances the mathematical content reading and writing experience.
segment.target: This NVDA addon enhances the mathematical content reading and writing experience.

segment.source: The reading feature includes improved language translation in the ability to segment long math content into smaller parts using interactive navigation.
segment.target: The reading feature includes improved language translation in the ability to segment long math content into smaller parts using interactive navigation.

segment.source: The writing feature includes a command menu for easier LaTeX/AsciiMath/Nemeth input and the ability to export to a visually readable HTML document for better collaboration.
segment.target: The writing feature includes a command menu for easier LaTeX/AsciiMath/Nemeth input and the ability to export to a visually readable HTML document for better collaboration.

segment.source: reading feature
segment.target: reading feature

segment.source: Reads entire lines including text and mathematical content
segment.target: Reads entire lines including text and mathematical content

segment.source: Provides interactive navigation to move, read, and zoom in or out on mathematical content snippets
segment.target: Provides interactive navigation to move, read, and zoom in or out on mathematical content snippets

segment.source: Allows for reviewing text while interactive navigation
segment.target: Allows for reviewing text while interactive navigation

segment.source: Uses simplified speech rules
segment.target: Uses simplified speech rules

segment.source: Breaks speech between items in mathematical content
segment.target: Breaks speech between items in mathematical content

segment.source: writing feature
segment.target: writing feature

segment.source: Allows writing of mathematical content using LaTeX/AsciiMath/Nemeth
segment.target: Allows writing of mathematical content using LaTeX/AsciiMath/Nemeth

segment.source: Provides a command menu for inputting LaTeX
segment.target: Provides a command menu for inputting LaTeX

segment.source: Offers shortcut gestures for inputting LaTeX
segment.target: Offers shortcut gestures for inputting LaTeX

segment.source: Allows for more efficient cursor movement during editing
segment.target: Allows for more efficient cursor movement during editing

segment.source: Allows reads documents containing LaTeX/AsciiMath/Nemeth data in real-time while editing the document
segment.target: Allows reads documents containing LaTeX/AsciiMath/Nemeth data in real-time while editing the document

segment.source: Provides converting text files into HTML files for preview and export.
segment.target: Provides converting text files into HTML files for preview and export.

segment.source: Access8Math ReadMe
segment.target: Access8Math ReadMe

segment.source: This NVDA add-on enhances speech and braille output for mathematical content. Although the NVDA already integrating MathPlayer, certain aspects require improvement, such as the lack or incompleteness of language-specific translation, language-specific interactive navigation.
segment.target: This NVDA add-on enhances speech and braille output for mathematical content. Although the NVDA already integrating MathPlayer, certain aspects require improvement, such as the lack or incompleteness of language-specific translation, language-specific interactive navigation.

segment.source: Interactive navigation dissects mathematical content into smaller portions for speech or braille output, allowing for the selection of the desired fragment and mode of delivery through keyboard commands. This enhances one's understanding of the structure and elements of lengthy mathematical content, and the hierarchical relationships between items.
segment.target: Interactive navigation dissects mathematical content into smaller portions for speech or braille output, allowing for the selection of the desired fragment and mode of delivery through keyboard commands. This enhances one's understanding of the structure and elements of lengthy mathematical content, and the hierarchical relationships between items.

segment.source: Additionally, this NVDA add-on enables the writing of mathematical content. Using the command menu, input can be executed without having to memorize LaTeX codes. Upon completion, the document can be transformed into a visually comprehensible HTML document via the view and export function, thereby enabling seamless collaboration and discourse with others.
segment.target: Additionally, this NVDA add-on enables the writing of mathematical content. Using the command menu, input can be executed without having to memorize LaTeX codes. Upon completion, the document can be transformed into a visually comprehensible HTML document via the view and export function, thereby enabling seamless collaboration and discourse with others.

segment.source: Reading feature
segment.target: Reading feature

segment.source: Access8Math is able to read MathML content in web browsers such as Mozilla Firefox, Google Chrome, and Microsoft Edge, as well as MathType content in Microsoft Word.
segment.target: Access8Math is able to read MathML content in web browsers such as Mozilla Firefox, Google Chrome, and Microsoft Edge, as well as MathType content in Microsoft Word.

segment.source: By pressing the space bar or enter key on a MathML math object, you can enter navigation mode and interact with the content by moving, and zooming in or out on reading snippets.
segment.target: By pressing the space bar or enter key on a MathML math object, you can enter navigation mode and interact with the content by moving, and zooming in or out on reading snippets.

segment.source: Analyze the overall mathematical meaning of the content: analyze the structure of MathML, and when it meets a specific rule, read it aloud in the mathematical meaning of the rule
segment.target: Analyze the overall mathematical meaning of the content: analyze the structure of MathML, and when it meets a specific rule, read it aloud in the mathematical meaning of the rule

segment.source: Analyze the mathematical meaning of the content item: When navigating and browsing, it will prompt the meaning of the content under its upper content. For example, there are two score items, and moving between them will enroll the item as the denominator or numerator
segment.target: Analyze the mathematical meaning of the content item: When navigating and browsing, it will prompt the meaning of the content under its upper content. For example, there are two score items, and moving between them will enroll the item as the denominator or numerator

segment.source: navigation interactive mode command：
segment.target: navigation interactive mode command：

segment.source: The following commands are available in navigation interactive mode:
segment.target: The following commands are available in navigation interactive mode:

segment.source: "Down Arrow": shrink the reading snippet(zoom in)
segment.target: "Down Arrow": shrink the reading snippet(zoom in)

segment.source: "Up Arrow": enlarge the reading snippet(zoom out)
segment.target: "Up Arrow": enlarge the reading snippet(zoom out)

segment.source: "Left Arrow": Moves to the previous math content.
segment.target: "Left Arrow": Moves to the previous math content.

segment.source: "Right Arrow": Moves to the next math content.
segment.target: "Right Arrow": Moves to the next math content.

segment.source: "home": Moves back to the top of the entire math content.
segment.target: "home": Moves back to the top of the entire math content.

segment.source: "ctrl+C": Copies the MathML source code of the object.
segment.target: "ctrl+C": Copies the MathML source code of the object.

segment.source: "numpad 1~9": Reads the math content as serialized text using NVDA Reviewing Text.
segment.target: "numpad 1~9": Reads the math content as serialized text using NVDA Reviewing Text.

segment.source: "ESC": Exits the navigation mode.
segment.target: "ESC": Exits the navigation mode.

segment.source: Writing feature
segment.target: Writing feature

segment.source: Comprehensive content: Incorporating Both Text and Mathematical Content
segment.target: Comprehensive content: Incorporating Both Text and Mathematical Content

segment.source: For Windows 11, the Access8Math editor must be used, while for Windows 10 or earlier versions, either Access8Math editor or Notepad can be used. It is recommended to use the built-in Access8Math editor for writing comprehensive content.
segment.target: For Windows 11, the Access8Math editor must be used, while for Windows 10 or earlier versions, either Access8Math editor or Notepad can be used. It is recommended to use the built-in Access8Math editor for writing comprehensive content.

segment.source: To distinguish between text content and mathematical content, you can use delimiters to enclose the mathematical notation area. This means that any data within the mathematical notation area will be considered mathematical content according to the specified delimiter, while data outside of the mathematical notation area will be treated as text content.
segment.target: To distinguish between text content and mathematical content, you can use delimiters to enclose the mathematical notation area. This means that any data within the mathematical notation area will be considered mathematical content according to the specified delimiter, while data outside of the mathematical notation area will be treated as text content.

segment.source: category | start delimiter | end delimiter
segment.target: category | start delimiter | end delimiter

segment.source: LaTeX(bracket) | \( | \)
segment.target: LaTeX(bracket) | \( | \)

segment.source: LaTeX(money) | $ | $
segment.target: LaTeX(money) | $ | $

segment.source: AsciiMath | ` | `
segment.target: AsciiMath | ` | `

segment.source: Nemeth(UEB) | _% | _:
segment.target: Nemeth(UEB) | _% | _:

segment.source: Nemeth(at) | @ | @
segment.target: Nemeth(at) | @ | @

segment.source: You can choose what delimiter use for LaTeX/Nemeth in the writing setting.
segment.target: You can choose what delimiter use for LaTeX/Nemeth in the writing setting.

segment.source: Command gesture (toggle: NVDA+alt+c)
segment.target: Command gesture (toggle: NVDA+alt+c)

segment.source: alt+m: The mark command window pops up, select LaTeX/AsciiMath/Nemeth and press enter to add LaTeX/AsciiMath marks before and after the currently selected text (the current cursor when no text is selected) and automatically move the cursor into it. Enter content quickly.
segment.target: alt+m: The mark command window pops up, select LaTeX/AsciiMath/Nemeth and press enter to add LaTeX/AsciiMath marks before and after the currently selected text (the current cursor when no text is selected) and automatically move the cursor into it. Enter content quickly.

segment.source: alt+l: Pop up the LaTeX command window, select the LaTeX command item to be added and press the enter key to add the corresponding LaTeX syntax at the current cursor and automatically move the cursor to the appropriate input point for quick input.
segment.target: alt+l: Pop up the LaTeX command window, select the LaTeX command item to be added and press the enter key to add the corresponding LaTeX syntax at the current cursor and automatically move the cursor to the appropriate input point for quick input.

segment.source: LaTeX command window
segment.target: LaTeX command window

segment.source: Move to any LaTeX command item and press English letters (a~z) or f1~f12 to set shortcuts
segment.target: Move to any LaTeX command item and press English letters (a~z) or f1~f12 to set shortcuts

segment.source: Move any LaTeX command item and press delete/back space to remove the shortcut that has been set
segment.target: Move any LaTeX command item and press delete/back space to remove the shortcut that has been set

segment.source: Move to any LaTeX command item and press enter to add the corresponding LaTeX syntax at the current cursor
segment.target: Move to any LaTeX command item and press enter to add the corresponding LaTeX syntax at the current cursor

segment.source: alt+i: When the cursor stops on the math block, you can interact with the math block for navigation
segment.target: alt+i: When the cursor stops on the math block, you can interact with the math block for navigation

segment.source: alt+h: HTML documents that can convert text data and mathematical data can be viewed or exported. The content of the math block is converted into MathML and presented side by side with the text to facilitate visual reading.
segment.target: alt+h: HTML documents that can convert text data and mathematical data can be viewed or exported. The content of the math block is converted into MathML and presented side by side with the text to facilitate visual reading.

segment.source: View: Open the converted HTML document through the default .HTML extension application set by the system.
segment.target: View: Open the converted HTML document through the default .HTML extension application set by the system.

segment.source: Export: Pack the converted HTML file and the original text file into a compressed file.
segment.target: Export: Pack the converted HTML file and the original text file into a compressed file.

segment.source: alt+t: The data of the block where the cursor is located can be converted between LaTeX and AsciiMath (the cursor needs to be in the LaTeX or AsciiMath block)
segment.target: alt+t: The data of the block where the cursor is located can be converted between LaTeX and AsciiMath (the cursor needs to be in the LaTeX or AsciiMath block)

segment.source: In the writing settings, you can choose whether to enable command gestures at startup. Press NVDA+alt+c in the editing area to enable or disable command gestures, which can be changed in input gestures.
segment.target: In the writing settings, you can choose whether to enable command gestures at startup. Press NVDA+alt+c in the editing area to enable or disable command gestures, which can be changed in input gestures.

segment.source: Writing block navigation gestures (toggle: NVDA+alt+n)
segment.target: Writing block navigation gestures (toggle: NVDA+alt+n)

segment.source: alt+Left arrow key: move to the start point of the previous data block
segment.target: alt+Left arrow key: move to the start point of the previous data block

segment.source: alt+Down arrow key: Only read the content of the current data block without moving
segment.target: alt+Down arrow key: Only read the content of the current data block without moving

segment.source: alt+right arrow: move to the start point of the next data block
segment.target: alt+right arrow: move to the start point of the next data block

segment.source: alt+home: move to the starting point of the current data block
segment.target: alt+home: move to the starting point of the current data block

segment.source: alt+end: move to the end point of the current data block
segment.target: alt+end: move to the end point of the current data block

segment.source: alt+shift+left arrow: move to the previous data block and select
segment.target: alt+shift+left arrow: move to the previous data block and select

segment.source: alt+shift+down key: do not move, only select the content of the current data block
segment.target: alt+shift+down key: do not move, only select the content of the current data block

segment.source: alt+shift+right arrow: move to the next data block and select
segment.target: alt+shift+right arrow: move to the next data block and select

segment.source: In the writing settings, you can choose whether to enable the block navigation gestures when starting. Press NVDA+alt+n in the editing area to enable or disable the block navigation gestures, which can be changed in the input gestures.
segment.target: In the writing settings, you can choose whether to enable the block navigation gestures when starting. Press NVDA+alt+n in the editing area to enable or disable the block navigation gestures, which can be changed in the input gestures.

segment.source: Shortcut gestures (toggle: NVDA+alt+s)
segment.target: Shortcut gestures (toggle: NVDA+alt+s)

segment.source: When the cursor is in the LaTeX block, press the letter, f1~f12 to quickly insert the bound LaTeX. Press shift+letter, shift+f1~f12 to read out the LaTeX currently bound to the shortcut. (Need to set the shortcut key in the command menu first)
segment.target: When the cursor is in the LaTeX block, press the letter, f1~f12 to quickly insert the bound LaTeX. Press shift+letter, shift+f1~f12 to read out the LaTeX currently bound to the shortcut. (Need to set the shortcut key in the command menu first)

segment.source: In the writing settings, you can choose whether to enable the quick navigation gestures at startup. Press NVDA+alt+s in the editing area to enable or disable the quick navigation gestures, which can be changed in the input gestures.
segment.target: In the writing settings, you can choose whether to enable the quick navigation gestures at startup. Press NVDA+alt+s in the editing area to enable or disable the quick navigation gestures, which can be changed in the input gestures.

segment.source: Greek letter gestures (toggle: NVDA+alt+g)
segment.target: Greek letter gestures (toggle: NVDA+alt+g)

segment.source: When the cursor is in the LaTeX block, press the letter to quickly insert the corresponding Greek letter LaTeX.
segment.target: When the cursor is in the LaTeX block, press the letter to quickly insert the corresponding Greek letter LaTeX.

segment.source: Browse navigation mode (toggle: NVDA+space)
segment.target: Browse navigation mode (toggle: NVDA+space)

segment.source: When the navigation mode is turned on, the mathematical data block read by the cursor mobile report will read the mathematical text content instead of the original grammatical data
segment.target: When the navigation mode is turned on, the mathematical data block read by the cursor mobile report will read the mathematical text content instead of the original grammatical data

segment.source: You can use the following key gestures to move the editing cursor and interactive navigation
segment.target: You can use the following key gestures to move the editing cursor and interactive navigation

segment.source: Left key: move to the starting point of the previous data block and read it out
segment.target: Left key: move to the starting point of the previous data block and read it out

segment.source: Right arrow: move to the start point of the next data block and read it out
segment.target: Right arrow: move to the start point of the next data block and read it out

segment.source: Up key: move to the previous line and read out the contents of all data blocks in this line
segment.target: Up key: move to the previous line and read out the contents of all data blocks in this line

segment.source: Down key: move to the next line and read out the contents of all data blocks in this line
segment.target: Down key: move to the next line and read out the contents of all data blocks in this line

segment.source: pageUp: move up ten lines and read out the content of all data blocks in that line
segment.target: pageUp: move up ten lines and read out the content of all data blocks in that line

segment.source: pageDown: move down ten lines and read out the content of all data blocks in that line
segment.target: pageDown: move down ten lines and read out the content of all data blocks in that line

segment.source: home: move to the first block of the line where the cursor is located
segment.target: home: move to the first block of the line where the cursor is located

segment.source: end: move to the last block of the line where the cursor is located
segment.target: end: move to the last block of the line where the cursor is located

segment.source: Use the cursor to move the key plus the shift key to select the text together
segment.target: Use the cursor to move the key plus the shift key to select the text together

segment.source: space/enter: When the cursor stops on a math block, it can interact with the math content of the data block to navigate
segment.target: space/enter: When the cursor stops on a math block, it can interact with the math content of the data block to navigate

segment.source: For the following keys, if you only press the single key, the editing cursor will jump to the next data block position, if you press shift + the single key at the same time, the editing cursor will jump to the previous data block position:
segment.target: For the following keys, if you only press the single key, the editing cursor will jump to the next data block position, if you press shift + the single key at the same time, the editing cursor will jump to the previous data block position:

segment.source: l: Move to the next LaTeX data block and read it out
segment.target: l: Move to the next LaTeX data block and read it out

segment.source: a: Move to the next AsciiMath data block and read it out
segment.target: a: Move to the next AsciiMath data block and read it out

segment.source: n: Move to the next Nemeth data block and read it out
segment.target: n: Move to the next Nemeth data block and read it out

segment.source: m: move to the next MathML data block and read
segment.target: m: move to the next MathML data block and read

segment.source: t: move to the next text block and read it
segment.target: t: move to the next text block and read it

segment.source: tab: move to the next interactive block (math block) and read it out
segment.target: tab: move to the next interactive block (math block) and read it out

segment.source: The following key gestures can be used to edit contents
segment.target: The following key gestures can be used to edit contents

segment.source: ctrl+x: cut the current cursor block
segment.target: ctrl+x: cut the current cursor block

segment.source: ctrl+c: copy the current cursor block
segment.target: ctrl+c: copy the current cursor block

segment.source: ctrl+v: Paste content after the current cursor block
segment.target: ctrl+v: Paste content after the current cursor block

segment.source: delete/back space: delete the current cursor block
segment.target: delete/back space: delete the current cursor block

segment.source: Access8Math editor and Access8Math Document
segment.target: Access8Math editor and Access8Math Document

segment.source: The edit area of Notepad in Windows 11 is rich editing area not traditional editing area, so Access8Math editor must be used in Windows 11 to use the writing feature. This editor provides such as opening old files, saving files, and view feature.
segment.target: The edit area of Notepad in Windows 11 is rich editing area not traditional editing area, so Access8Math editor must be used in Windows 11 to use the writing feature. This editor provides such as opening old files, saving files, and view feature.

segment.source: Use the Access8Math editor to write markdown documents, and when document have some resource, the resources can put into workspace of editor and be referenced. When using export feature of the editor, the referenced resources in the document will be packaged into a Archive file by Access8Math editor.
segment.target: Use the Access8Math editor to write markdown documents, and when document have some resource, the resources can put into workspace of editor and be referenced. When using export feature of the editor, the referenced resources in the document will be packaged into a Archive file by Access8Math editor.

segment.source: The preview and export feature under the view menu are the same as the command gesture view feature (alt+h), which can generate an Access8Math Document. The only difference between the two is that the Access8Math editor will package referenced resources into a archive file
segment.target: The preview and export feature under the view menu are the same as the command gesture view feature (alt+h), which can generate an Access8Math Document. The only difference between the two is that the Access8Math editor will package referenced resources into a archive file

segment.source: In order to allow the document exported by the Access8Math editor to be imported again and edited, the Access8Math editor will write a Access8Math.json when exporting, which is the meta data. We call the archive file/folder containing this meta is Access8Math Document
segment.target: In order to allow the document exported by the Access8Math editor to be imported again and edited, the Access8Math editor will write a Access8Math.json when exporting, which is the meta data. We call the archive file/folder containing this meta is Access8Math Document

segment.source: In File Explorer, you can press the NVDA+application, if the selected path is txt/Access8Math Document, a virtual context menu will open, which can quickly open this file to view or edit it.
segment.target: In File Explorer, you can press the NVDA+application, if the selected path is txt/Access8Math Document, a virtual context menu will open, which can quickly open this file to view or edit it.

segment.source: content example:
segment.target: content example:

segment.source: LaTeX(bracket): The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable \(ax^2+bx+c=0\) is \(\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a}\) .
segment.target: LaTeX(bracket): The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable \(ax^2+bx+c=0\) is \(\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a}\) .

segment.source: LaTeX(dollar): The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable $ax^2+bx+c=0$ is $\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a}$ .
segment.target: LaTeX(dollar): The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable $ax^2+bx+c=0$ is $\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a}$ .

segment.source: AsciiMath: The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable `ax^2+bx+c=0` is `x=(-b+- \sqrt(b^2-4ac))/(2a)` .
segment.target: AsciiMath: The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable `ax^2+bx+c=0` is `x=(-b+- \sqrt(b^2-4ac))/(2a)` .

segment.source: Nemeth(UEB): The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable _%⠁⠭⠘⠆⠐⠬⠃⠭⠬⠉⠀⠨⠅⠀⠴_: is _%⠭⠀⠨⠅⠀⠹⠤⠃⠬⠤⠜⠃⠘⠆⠐⠤⠲⠁⠉⠻⠌⠆⠁⠼_: .
segment.target: Nemeth(UEB): The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable _%⠁⠭⠘⠆⠐⠬⠃⠭⠬⠉⠀⠨⠅⠀⠴_: is _%⠭⠀⠨⠅⠀⠹⠤⠃⠬⠤⠜⠃⠘⠆⠐⠤⠲⠁⠉⠻⠌⠆⠁⠼_: .

segment.source: Nemeth(at): The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable @⠁⠭⠘⠆⠐⠬⠃⠭⠬⠉⠀⠨⠅⠀⠴@ is @⠭⠀⠨⠅⠀⠹⠤⠃⠬⠤⠜⠃⠘⠆⠐⠤⠲⠁⠉⠻⠌⠆⠁⠼@ .
segment.target: Nemeth(at): The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable @⠁⠭⠘⠆⠐⠬⠃⠭⠬⠉⠀⠨⠅⠀⠴@ is @⠭⠀⠨⠅⠀⠹⠤⠃⠬⠤⠜⠃⠘⠆⠐⠤⠲⠁⠉⠻⠌⠆⠁⠼@ .

segment.source: MathML: The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable <math display="inline"><mi>a</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math display="inline"><mfrac><mrow><mo>−</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>±</mi><msqrt><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>−</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>c</mi></msqrt></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> .
segment.target: MathML: The solution of the quadratic equation in one variable <math display="inline"><mi>a</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math display="inline"><mfrac><mrow><mo>−</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>±</mi><msqrt><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>−</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>c</mi></msqrt></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> .

segment.source: All Access8Math settings are centralized in tools -> Access8Math->Settings
segment.target: All Access8Math settings are centralized in tools -> Access8Math->Settings

segment.source: Reading Settings
segment.target: Reading Settings

segment.source: Language: Access8Math read language
segment.target: Language: Access8Math read language

segment.source: Analyze mathematical meaning of content: perform semantic analysis on the mathematical content, and when it meets a specific rule, using that rule to speak.
segment.target: Analyze mathematical meaning of content: perform semantic analysis on the mathematical content, and when it meets a specific rule, using that rule to speak.

segment.source: show interaction window when entering interaction navigation mode: Whether to show "Access8Math interaction window" when active event entering interaction navigation mode on math object
segment.target: show interaction window when entering interaction navigation mode: Whether to show "Access8Math interaction window" when active event entering interaction navigation mode on math object

segment.source: Reading pre-defined meaning in dictionary in interaction navigation mode: When the pattern is definied in the dictionary, use dictionary to read the meaning of subpart in the upper layer part.
segment.target: Reading pre-defined meaning in dictionary in interaction navigation mode: When the pattern is definied in the dictionary, use dictionary to read the meaning of subpart in the upper layer part.

segment.source: Reading of auto-generated meaning in interaction navigation mode: When the pattern is not difined or incomplete in dictionary, use automatic generation function to read the meaning of subpart in the upper layer part.
segment.target: Reading of auto-generated meaning in interaction navigation mode: When the pattern is not difined or incomplete in dictionary, use automatic generation function to read the meaning of subpart in the upper layer part.

segment.source: use tone indicate to no move in interaction navigation mode
segment.target: use tone indicate to no move in interaction navigation mode

segment.source: Item interval time: Setting break time between items. Values from 1 to 100, the smaller the value, the shorter the break time, and the greater the value, the longer the break time.
segment.target: Item interval time: Setting break time between items. Values from 1 to 100, the smaller the value, the shorter the break time, and the greater the value, the longer the break time.

segment.source: localization
segment.target: localization

segment.source: If you want Access8Math to have speech/braille output for MathML in different languages, you need to create a Unicode dictionary (unicode.dic) and mathematical rules (math.rule). This can be done using the GUI in Access8Math -> Localization, or by referring to the globalPlugins/Access8Math/locale in the plugin folder.
segment.target: If you want Access8Math to have speech/braille output for MathML in different languages, you need to create a Unicode dictionary (unicode.dic) and mathematical rules (math.rule). This can be done using the GUI in Access8Math -> Localization, or by referring to the globalPlugins/Access8Math/locale in the plugin folder.

segment.source: The "Unicode Dictionary" enables you to customize speech or braille output for symbols and characters. The "Mathematics Rules" allow you to customize speech or braille output for different types of mathematics.
segment.target: The "Unicode Dictionary" enables you to customize speech or braille output for symbols and characters. The "Mathematics Rules" allow you to customize speech or braille output for different types of mathematics.

segment.source: "New language" dialog can adding language not included in Access8Math built-in. Upon Adding language option will appear in the Reading Settings language selection, Allowing for the Definition of speech/braille via the Unicode Dictionary and Mathematics Rules, Resulting in Localization.
segment.target: "New language" dialog can adding language not included in Access8Math built-in. Upon Adding language option will appear in the Reading Settings language selection, Allowing for the Definition of speech/braille via the Unicode Dictionary and Mathematics Rules, Resulting in Localization.

segment.source: Math Rules
segment.target: Math Rules

segment.source: Access8Math establishes mathematical rules according to the mathematical type and logic to decide the reading math method and order. According to different local math reading logic, the math reading text and order can be changed. The method is as follows:
segment.target: Access8Math establishes mathematical rules according to the mathematical type and logic to decide the reading math method and order. According to different local math reading logic, the math reading text and order can be changed. The method is as follows:

segment.source: Edit: After entering the "math rule", the window lists 46 math rules. Choose any math rule and select the "Edit" to enter the editing entry.
segment.target: Edit: After entering the "math rule", the window lists 46 math rules. Choose any math rule and select the "Edit" to enter the editing entry.

segment.source: The "editing entry" can be divided into two major blocks, the "Serialized ordering" and the "Child role".
segment.target: The "editing entry" can be divided into two major blocks, the "Serialized ordering" and the "Child role".

segment.source: Serialized ordering: Math rule is divided into multiple blocks according to the reading order. In this area, the reading order of child node and the delimitation text of start, inter- and the end can be changed. Taking the fractional rule mfrac as an example, this rule is divided into five reading blocks. The order 0, 2, and 4 represent the initial prompt, the project segmentation prompt, and the end prompt, respectively, and the meanings text can be changed in each field. Order 1 and 3 adjust the reading sequence of child node which can be changed in the drop-down menu.
segment.target: Serialized ordering: Math rule is divided into multiple blocks according to the reading order. In this area, the reading order of child node and the delimitation text of start, inter- and the end can be changed. Taking the fractional rule mfrac as an example, this rule is divided into five reading blocks. The order 0, 2, and 4 represent the initial prompt, the project segmentation prompt, and the end prompt, respectively, and the meanings text can be changed in each field. Order 1 and 3 adjust the reading sequence of child node which can be changed in the drop-down menu.

segment.source: Child role: The next-level sub-item of the mathematical rule. Taking the fractional rule mfrac as an example, the rule contains the numerator and the denominator. The sub-content in the upper sub-content meaning can be changed in the child-node role field.
segment.target: Child role: The next-level sub-item of the mathematical rule. Taking the fractional rule mfrac as an example, the rule contains the numerator and the denominator. The sub-content in the upper sub-content meaning can be changed in the child-node role field.

segment.source: Example: You can check the reading method of this math rule after editing. After clicking, a math content is preset the corresponding math rules for confirming whether the reading method is as expected.
segment.target: Example: You can check the reading method of this math rule after editing. After clicking, a math content is preset the corresponding math rules for confirming whether the reading method is as expected.

segment.source: Recover default: Restores the list of math rules to their initial presets.
segment.target: Recover default: Restores the list of math rules to their initial presets.

segment.source: Import: Import math rules files, which can be used to load math rules files.
segment.target: Import: Import math rules files, which can be used to load math rules files.

segment.source: Export: Save the math rules file to the specified path to share or keep.
segment.target: Export: Save the math rules file to the specified path to share or keep.

segment.source: New rule
segment.target: New rule

segment.source: positive rule: Read "positive" rather than "plus" when plus sign in first item or its previous item is certain operator.
segment.target: positive rule: Read "positive" rather than "plus" when plus sign in first item or its previous item is certain operator.

segment.source: square rule: When the power is 2, the item is read as "squared".
segment.target: square rule: When the power is 2, the item is read as "squared".

segment.source: cubic rule: When the power is 3, the item is read as "cubed".
segment.target: cubic rule: When the power is 3, the item is read as "cubed".

segment.source: line rule: When there is "↔" right above two Identifier, the item is read as "Line ...".
segment.target: line rule: When there is "↔" right above two Identifier, the item is read as "Line ...".

segment.source: line segment rule: When there is "¯" right above two Identifier, the item is read as "Line segement ...".
segment.target: line segment rule: When there is "¯" right above two Identifier, the item is read as "Line segement ...".

segment.source: ray rule: When there is a "→" right above two Identifier, the item is read as "Ray ..."
segment.target: ray rule: When there is a "→" right above two Identifier, the item is read as "Ray ..."
